vulnerability.title: Add nodev Option to Non-Root Local Partitions
vulnerability.description: The nodev mount option prevents files from being interpreted as character or block devices. Legitimate character and block devices should exist only in the /dev directory on the root partition or within chroot jails built for system services. Add the nodev option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of any non-root local partitions.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then MOUNT_OPTION="nodev" # Create array of local non-root partitions readarray -t partitions_records < <(findmnt --mtab --raw --evaluate | grep "^/\w" | grep -v "^/proc" | grep "\s/dev/\w") # Create array of polyinstantiated directories, in case one of them is found in mtab readarray -t polyinstantiated_dirs < \ <(grep -oP "^\s*[^#\s]+\s+\S+" /etc/security/namespace.conf | grep -oP "(?<=\s)\S+?(?=/?\$)") # Define excluded non-local file systems excluded_fstypes=( afs autofs ceph cif

triage_decision.reason: The nodev mount option is a low-risk change that prevents files from being interpreted as character or block devices, and is not related to authn/authz, networking, or high-risk filesystem changes.

execution_details.stdout:
#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info.
#
# After editing this file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' to update systemd
# units generated from this file.
#
/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults        0 0,nodev,nodev,nodev
UUID=F56C-228D          /boot/efi               vfat    umask=0077,shortname=winnt 0 2,nodev,nodev
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs nodev,nosuid,noexec 0 0

llm_verdict.message:
A previous command modified the file object.
Note: Results may vary. Depending on the system, some commands may not be necessary.

review_feedback: The remediation approach is fragile and incomplete, with potential for syntax errors in /etc/fstab that could prevent boot or cause other issues. Suggestions: Use findmnt or blkid to enumerate non-root local partitions and generate the correct fstab lines.; Replace the series of sed invocations with a single, idempotent operation that adds nodev only where appropriate.; Validate the resulting /etc/fstab with 'mount -o –test=parse' or similar before applying.; Ensure the change is made in a transactional manner to allow rollback on failure.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation adds the nodev option to selected mount points but does so with fragile sed commands and incomplete targeting of non-root partitions.
concerns: Multiple sed commands risk duplicating or incorrectly modifying fstab entries.
Targeting is limited to specific UUIDs and /home, not all non-root local partitions.
Potential for syntax errors in /etc/fstab that could prevent boot.
No validation step to ensure the changes are syntactically correct or applied only to local filesystems.
suggested_improvements: Use findmnt or blkid to enumerate non-root local partitions and generate the correct fstab lines.
Replace the series of sed invocations with a single, idempotent operation that adds nodev only where appropriate.
Validate the resulting /etc/fstab with 'mount -o –test=parse' or similar before applying.
Ensure the change is made in a transactional manner to allow rollback on failure.
